target.title: fix(agent): surface non_deliverable_terminal_turn with specific message and auto-retry
target.body:
## Summary

When a model turn ends with `stop_reason: tool_use` but no explicit terminal delivery, the runtime classifies it as `non_deliverable_terminal_turn`. Previously, this `terminalError` was only recorded in trajectory metadata and never surfaced to the user. The user would see the generic "Something went wrong… use /new" message, which gives misleading remediation advice and hides the actual failure.

## Changes

1. **Propagate `trajectoryTerminalError`** from attempt trajectory through `EmbeddedAgentRunMeta` so the reply layer can see it
2. **Auto-retry once** for `non_deliverable_terminal_turn` — the model produced no output, so retry is idempotent and usually resolves the issue
3. **Surface a specific, actionable message** when retry is exhausted

## Files changed

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — add `trajectoryTerminalError` to return
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts` — add type for `trajectoryTerminalError`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts` — add to `EmbeddedAgentRunMeta`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` — propagate through meta
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-failure-copy.ts` — add user-facing message constant
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` — auto-retry + specific message
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` — 3 new tests

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `non_deliverable_terminal_turn` (stop_reason=tool_use, no delivery) surfaced as generic "Something went wrong… use /new" with no diagnostic signal and no retry. The fix propagates the terminal error to the reply layer, auto-retries once, and surfaces a specific actionable message on exhaustion.

Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw main branch (commit d1ab308f5c).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `pnpm build` — clean build, exit 0
2. `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-trajectory-status` — 19/19 passed
3. `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution` — 207/207 passed (204 existing + 3 new: auto-retry, exhausted retry message, no retry when fallback exhausted)
4. `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier` — passed
5. `pnpm test src/trajectory/export` — passed
6. `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/` — 277/298 passed (21 failures pre-existing in unrelated auth/failover/setup)
7. `pnpm test src/auto-reply/` — 297/339 passed (agent-runner-execution.test.ts: 207/207)

Evidence after fix:
- New tests verify 3 code paths: (a) auto-retry triggers when trajectoryTerminalError === "non_deliverable_terminal_turn" with no visible payloads — embedded runner called twice, second succeeds; (b) exhausted retry returns kind: "final" with NON_DELIVERABLE_TERMINAL_TURN_USER_TEXT: "⚠️ The model stopped between tool calls without producing a reply. Please try the same message again — this usually resolves on retry. If it persists, use /new or a lighter model."; (c) fallback-exhausted path skips retry and still surfaces the specific message instead of generic text.
- trajectoryTerminalError field flows: resolveAttemptTrajectoryTerminal → attempt.ts return → run.ts meta → EmbeddedAgentRunMeta → agent-runner-execution.ts reply layer
- Auto-retry uses consumeNonDeliverableTurnRetry() with 1-retry budget (same pattern as consumeTransientHttpRetry)

Observed result after fix:
- All 207 agent-runner-execution tests pass with no regressions
- Type checker passes on changed files (pnpm tsgo)
- Build succeeds (pnpm build, exit 0)
- When trajectoryTerminalError === "non_deliverable_terminal_turn" with empty payloads, the code auto-retries once before surfacing the specific message

What was not tested: Full end-to-end reproduction with Telegram DM under heavy cacheRead load. Requires production-like environment with live API keys, sustained load, and a provider model to trigger the race between stop_reason=tool_use and turn closure. Unit tests cover the code paths; Crabbox E2E would be needed for live roundtrip proof.

target.title: perf(agents): parallelize MCP and LSP initialization to reduce cold-start latency
target.body:
## Summary

Every agent request incurs ~6-7 seconds of latency before the model starts generating. The bottleneck is the `bundle-tools` phase which loads all MCP servers and LSP tools upfront. The root cause: MCP server connections, LSP server spawning, and their coordination are all **sequential**.

## Root Cause

1. **MCP connections sequential**: `getCatalog()` iterates servers one at a time with a `for` loop
2. **LSP spawning sequential**: `createBundleLspToolRuntime()` spawns LSP servers one at a time
3. **MCP and LSP sequential**: `attempt.ts` awaits MCP completion before starting LSP

Trace evidence (from production, before fix):
```
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: totalMs=5889 stages=bundle-tools:5792ms@5809ms
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: totalMs=7207 stages=bundle-tools:7132ms@7207ms
```
`bundle-tools` accounts for **96-99% of total prep time**.

## Fix

### 1. Parallelize MCP server connections (`agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`)

`getCatalog()`: Phase 1 (sync resolve) → Phase 2 (`Promise.all` parallel connect) → Phase 3 (assemble)

### 2. Parallelize LSP server initialization (`agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.ts`)

`createBundleLspToolRuntime()`: Phase 1 (sync validate) → Phase 2 (`Promise.allSettled` parallel spawn) → Phase 3 (assemble)

### 3. Run MCP and LSP in parallel (`attempt.ts`)

`Promise.all([mcpTask.catch(), lspTask.catch()])` replaces sequential `await mcp; await lsp`. Each catches own errors.

### 4. Preserve cross-runtime tool-name dedup (`attempt.ts`)

Post-hoc dedup after parallel init filters LSP tools colliding with MCP tool names.

## Expected Improvement

4 MCP servers at ~1-2s each: **Before ~6-8s → After ~1.5-2s** (max of servers, not sum).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The `bundle-tools` prep stage takes 6-7s on every agent request (96-99% of total prep time) because MCP server connections, `tools/list` calls, LSP server spawning, and MCP/LSP coordination all run sequentially in `for` loops and serial `await` chains.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw @ a615a0ba09 (this branch). Real `createSessionMcpRuntime` with real `getCatalog()` parallel code path. Mock MCP servers are real Node.js child processes speaking the MCP JSON-RPC protocol over stdin/stdout — the same transport used by production stdio MCP servers.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `node --import tsx scripts/verify-parallel-mcp.mts` — 2 MCP servers, 250ms tools/list delay each
  2. `node --import tsx scripts/verify-parallel-3srv.mts` — 3 MCP servers, 200ms tools/list delay each
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts` — 33 tests passed
  4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.test.ts` — 3 tests passed
  5. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` — 122 tests passed

- **Evidence after fix**:

  **Local verification run 1** — 2 servers × 250ms delay:
  ```
  ============================================================
    OpenClaw Parallel MCP Verification
  ============================================================
  MCP servers: 2
  Per-server tools/list delay: 250ms
  Sequential expected: 500ms
  Parallel expected: ~250ms (max of servers)

  Calling getCatalog() ...
  getCatalog() returned in 339ms
  Servers in catalog: 2
  Tools discovered: 2
    - verify_tool_0 (from verifySrv0)
    - verify_tool_1 (from verifySrv1)

  Result:
    Tools found: 2/2 ✓
    Wall time: 339ms (sequential would be ~500ms+)
    Parallel threshold: <425ms ✓

  ✓✓✓ PARALLELISM CONFIRMED ✓✓✓
  ============================================================
  ```

  **Local verification run 2** — 3 servers × 200ms delay:
  ```
  === 3-server verification: 3 servers × 200ms delay ===
  Sequential expected: 600ms | Parallel expected: ~200ms

  getCatalog() = 291ms | Tools: 3/3
  Servers: 3
    vt3_0 (vs3_0)
    vt3_1 (vs3_1)
    vt3_2 (vs3_2)

  ✓✓✓ PARALLEL: 291ms < 480ms threshold
  ```

  **Code-level proof**:
  - `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`: sequential `for` loop → `Promise.all(serverPlans.map(...))`
  - `agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.ts`: sequential `for` loop → `Promise.allSettled(serverPlans.map(...))`
  - `attempt.ts`: serial `await mcp; await lsp` → `Promise.all([mcpTask.catch(), lspTask.catch()])`
  - `attempt.ts`: post-hoc cross-runtime tool-name dedup (lines 1598-1619)

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - **Run 1**: 2 servers × 250ms → 339ms wall time (vs 500ms+ sequential). **2.0x speedup** (parallelism confirmed).
  - **Run 2**: 3 servers × 200ms → 291ms wall time (vs 600ms+ sequential). **2.1x speedup** (parallelism confirmed).
  - Both runs discovered all tools from all servers — correctness preserved.
  - Wall-clock time scales with `max(server_i)` not `sum(server_i)`. For 4 production servers at ~1.5s each: ~1.5s instead of ~6s.
  - MCP and LSP init now overlap via `Promise.all`.
  - Session-scoped MCP runtime caching is unchanged.
  - All 158 tests pass with zero regressions.

- **What was not tested**:
  - Live end-to-end trace with real networked MCP servers (Notion, Z.ai, Web Reader) — requires a production OpenClaw setup with actual API credentials.
  - The mock servers use local stdio transport (same protocol as production stdio MCP servers). Networked servers would show even larger relative gains since network RTT typically dominates.

## Files Changed

- `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts` — Parallelize MCP catalog connections
- `src/agents/agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.ts` — Parallelize LSP server initialization
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — Parallelize MCP+LSP coordination + cross-runtime dedup

target.title: fix(sandbox): skip read-only skill mounts when user binds target the same container path
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw.json` `sandbox.docker.binds` entries targeting reserved container paths (e.g. `/workspace/.agents/skills`) cause the Docker daemon to reject container creation with `Duplicate mount point`. The internal `resolveReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts` unconditionally appends read-only skill mounts without checking whether a user-defined bind already targets the same container path.
- Why now: Any user with custom docker binds that happen to overlap a skill mount path cannot launch sandbox containers at all. The gateway fails before the agent can start.
- What changed:
  - New `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds()` in `workspace-mounts.ts` parses user bind specs and excludes skill mounts whose container path is already targeted
  - `ensureSandboxContainer` applies the filter before config hash computation and container creation
  - `ensureSandboxBrowser` applies the filter before browser container creation
  - `buildSandboxFsMounts` applies the filter to keep the FS path resolution table consistent
- What did NOT change: Guard mounts in `agent-tools.ts` are unchanged — they don't generate Docker `-v` args. The `appendWorkspaceMountArgs` fallback path is untouched because production callers always pass `includeReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts: false`. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: User binds that conflict with skill mount paths no longer cause `Duplicate mount point`. User binds take precedence. Config hashes are computed from the effective mount list.
- Reviewer focus: `workspace-mounts.ts` `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds` function, and the three integration points in `docker.ts`, `browser.ts`, `fs-paths.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #93854

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issue has clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts` unconditionally appends internal read-only skill mounts for `rw` workspaces. When a user bind targets the same container path, `appendReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountArgs` adds a second `-v` spec for the same Docker target, and the daemon rejects the container.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No pre-existing check for container-path overlap between user binds and internal skill mounts. The project's comment-based mount ordering tests assumed Docker would accept duplicate targets and "last one wins", but Docker 29.x rejects duplicate mount points outright.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw.json` `sandbox.docker.binds` with `/host/custom-skills:/workspace/skills:rw` must not produce a second `-v ...:/workspace/skills:ro,z` — that second mount would cause Docker to reject the container with `Duplicate mount point`.
- Real environment tested: Linux x64, Node 22.19.0, pnpm 11.2.2, branch `fix/sandbox-bind-conflict`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs
  pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ node scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs
  Workspace: /tmp/openclaw-proof-h4CJtd

  Resolved skill mounts (no binds):
    /tmp/openclaw-proof-h4CJtd/skills -> /workspace/skills

  User binds: [ '/host/custom-skills:/workspace/skills:rw' ]

  Filtered skill mounts (after skipping conflicts):
    (none — conflicting mount was filtered out)

  Docker -v args that WOULD be generated (one per mount):
    -v /tmp/openclaw-proof-h4CJtd/skills:/workspace/skills:ro,z

  Docker -v args that ARE generated (after filtering):

  Conflicting mount at /workspace/skills in filtered output: false
  ✅ PASS: Conflicting skill mount filtered out — no Duplicate mount point from Docker

  $ pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/
   Test Files  22 passed (22)
        Tests  224 passed (224)
     Start at  17:40:41
     Duration  9.90s
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The conflicting `/workspace/skills` skill mount is absent from the filtered output. Docker receives only the user's bind, avoiding the `Duplicate mount point` error. All 224 sandbox tests pass.
- What was not tested: Live end-to-end Docker container creation against a real Docker 29.x daemon. The proof shows the function correctly filters the mount list that would be passed to `docker create`.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Sandbox environment without a Docker daemon — the proof exercises the exact `-v` argument list that would be sent to Docker.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 8 new unit tests for `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds`:
  1. `returns all mounts when binds is undefined`
  2. `returns all mounts when binds is empty`
  3. `filters out a mount whose container path matches a user bind`
  4. `filters out multiple mounts when multiple binds conflict`
  5. `returns all mounts when no bind targets conflict`
  6. `filters out all mounts when every container path is targeted by a bind`
  7. `handles rw binds (no ro option) correctly`
  8. `normalizes container paths before comparing`
- Updated 2 existing integration tests:
  1. `docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`: asserts conflicting mount is absent instead of appended last
  2. `browser.create.test.ts`: same update for the browser sandbox path
- Added 1 live proof script: `scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs`

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Docker daemon returned `Duplicate mount point` error when a user bind targeted the same container path as an internal skill mount. Container creation failed entirely — the agent could not start.

target.title: fix(media): resolve absolute-path channel media for native vision blocks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks. The model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image. The same workflow works correctly through the webchat / Control UI.
- Why now: Any user sending images via Telegram DM to an OpenClaw bot with a vision-capable model cannot get the agent to see or describe the image. The image file is correctly downloaded to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` but is silently dropped during native vision block hydration.
- What changed:
  - `MediaAttachmentCache.resolveLocalPath()` now validates absolute filesystem paths against local roots via `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`, matching the existing relative-path validation added in 610691c796
  - `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` now returns successfully resolved images even when a subset fails, instead of discarding the entire batch
  - Added regression test for absolute-path media hydration
- What did NOT change: Relative-path handling is unchanged. Webchat inline-image path (early-return on `params.images`) is untouched. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: Channel-delivered absolute-path images (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) are now hydrated into native vision content blocks instead of being silently dropped on a single read failure.
- Reviewer focus: `attachments.cache.ts` changes to `resolveLocalPath`, `current-turn-images.ts` changes to the all-or-nothing fallback logic, and the new test case.

## Linked context

closes #93816 Closes #79162, closes #62292, closes #69808

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issues have clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveLocalPath()` skipped absolute-path validation — it returned `path.resolve(rawPath)` directly while relative paths went through `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`. Channel-delivered inbound media (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) use absolute filesystem paths from `saveMediaBuffer`. When the path later failed `isInboundPathAllowed` in `ensureLocalStat`, the error was silently caught — channel attachments have `url` cleared, so `getBuffer` had no fallback and re-threw. Separately, `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` discarded all images when any single read failed.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test coverage for absolute-path media hydration. The existing test only covered relative paths. The symmetric relative-path validation in `resolveLocalPath` (added in 610691c796) was the guardrail that should have also covered absolute paths.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks; the model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04 (x64), Node 22.22.0, OpenClaw main branch (9166165).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs output:
  ✓ hydrates Telegram-style state-relative media into native prompt images
  ✓ hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  2 passed (18)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The new absolute-path test passes alongside the existing relative-path test. resolveCurrentTurnImages now returns partially-resolved image batches instead of discarding all images on a single failure. The absolute-path validation in resolveLocalPath is symmetric with the existing relative-path handling.
- What was not tested: Not run against a live Telegram bot with a real image. The proof exercises the full hydration chain through the media store with local file I/O.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Unit-test-based — does not verify a live end-to-end Telegram roundtrip.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media-understanding/runner.attachments.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 1 new test case:
  1. `hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images` — writes a file under a temp state/media directory, sets `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and asserts absolute-path hydration into native `ImageContent` blocks

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Telegram inbound images appeared only as `<media:image>` placeholder text
- Any channel using `saveMediaBuffer` for inbound media could silently lose native vision blocks when a single attachment read failed

commands.author: chenyangjun-xy

target.title: fix(embedding): resilient dimensions retry for openai-compatible self-hosted servers
target.body:
## Summary

When the openai-compatible embedding provider is configured with `outputDimensionality` and pointed at a self-hosted or third-party embedding server (llama.cpp, Ollama, Alibaba, etc.), the `dimensions` parameter causes the server to reject or connection-reset the request. This triggers the memory embedding retry+split cascade, logging "batch start" repeatedly without progress, and the index stalls indefinitely at 0/N files.

## Changes

1. **Try-with, retry-without dimensions** — `postEmbeddingRequest` always sends `dimensions` on the first attempt (preserving the configured `outputDimensionality` for compatible endpoints). On clear HTTP rejection (4xx with "dimensions"/"unknown parameter" in body) or connection reset (ECONNRESET, socket hang up, etc.), it automatically retries without the field. Pattern-based detection avoids hostname allowlists that break proxies.
2. **Batch completion logging** — `embedBatchWithRetry` and `embedBatchInputsWithRetry` now emit `batch completed` / `structured batch completed` debug logs after each successful batch, matching the existing `batch start` log.

## Files changed

- `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts` — add `DIMENSIONS_REJECTION_RE`, `DIMENSIONS_RESET_RE`, `buildEmbeddingRequestBody`, `doEmbeddingFetch`; rewrite `postEmbeddingRequest` with try-with, retry-without gate
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts` — add `batch completed` / `structured batch completed` debug logs
- `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` — new test "retries without dimensions when the server rejects the parameter" with dynamic per-request HTTP status callback
- `scripts/proof-embedding-retry.mjs` — live proof script exercising the production provider-adapter path against a simulated self-hosted server

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openai-compatible memory embedding against self-hosted servers (llama.cpp, Ollama) that reject the OpenAI `dimensions` parameter. Both batch and single query embedding send dimensions first, get HTTP 400, then retry without dimensions and succeed. The retry is transparent to the cache layer — `cacheKeyData` always includes dimensions regardless of wire behavior.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8 x86_64, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw main branch at d1ab308f5c, branch fix/openai-compatible-embedding-dimensions. Production `openAICompatibleEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create()` code path exercised against a real HTTP server simulating llama.cpp (returns 400 with `{"error":{"message":"Unrecognized request parameter: dimensions"}}` when `dimensions` is present, 200 with valid embeddings otherwise).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `pnpm test src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` — 25/25 passed (1 new: retries without dimensions when the server rejects the parameter)
2. `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.test.ts` — 14/14 passed
3. `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts` — 2/2 passed
4. `node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-embedding-retry.mjs` — 8/8 checks passed

Evidence after fix (real terminal output from step 4):
```
============================================================
  Real behavior proof: openai-compatible dimensions retry
  Simulated self-hosted llama.cpp / Ollama server
============================================================
Date:       2026-06-19T09:57:14.830Z
Runtime:    Node.js v22.19.0
Server:     http://127.0.0.1:38263/v1
Config:     provider=openai-compatible model=bge-m3.gguf outputDimensionality=1024
Server:     HTTP 400 on 'dimensions', 200 without

Provider:   id=openai-compatible model=bge-m3.gguf dims=1024
Cache key:  dimensions in identity = true

--- Batch embedding (3 documents) ---
Vectors:    3 (dim 3)
[25, 27, 13]

--- Query embedding ---
Vector:     [21, 22, 23]

--- Server request log ---
  #1: batch hasDims=true n=3 → 400→retry
  #2: batch hasDims=false n=3 → 200✓
  #3: query hasDims=true n=1 → 400→retry
  #4: query hasDims=false n=1 → 200✓

--- Verification ---
  [PASS] Batch sends dimensions first
  [PASS] Batch retries without dimensions
  [PASS] Query sends dimensions first
  [PASS] Query retries without dimensions
  [PASS] Batch returns 3 vectors
  [PASS] Query returns 3-dim vector
  [PASS] Cache includes dimensions
  [PASS] Exactly 4 requests total

ALL 8 CHECKS PASSED
============================================================
```

Observed result after fix:
- Production provider-adapter path sends `dimensions` on the first request (compatible endpoints unchanged). On HTTP 400 with "Unrecognized request parameter: dimensions", `DIMENSIONS_REJECTION_RE` matches and the request retries without dimensions, which succeeds.
- Both batch (3 documents) and single query paths verified — 4 total HTTP requests across 2 logical operations, each retrying exactly once.
- `cacheKeyData` preserves `dimensions` in the identity regardless of wire retry.
- 41 tests pass (25 + 14 + 2) with no regressions. TypeScript type check passes on changed files.

What was not tested: Live llama.cpp GPU server with BGE-M3 model (needs Intel Arc A770 not available in this environment). Alibaba Cloud text-embedding-v3 end-to-end (needs cloud credentials). Full multi-file memory index of 665 workspace files (needs complete OpenClaw workspace + GPU server). The unit tests and proof script cover the exact code paths from the bug report; Crabbox E2E would be needed for live roundtrip proof with a real embedding server.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Original contributor: @ZengWen-DT.

target.title: fix(i18n): improve Catalan localization (app + website)
target.body:
## Summary

Comprehensive review and improvement of the Catalan (`ca`) localization across both the app (`ca.lproj/Localizable.strings`) and the website (`docs/site-locales.mjs`).

### App strings (`Sources/CodexBar/Resources/ca.lproj/Localizable.strings`)

- **Switch address form from 'tu' to 'vós'** following Softcatalà guidelines, across imperatives, possessives, and verbal forms (e.g. `Desa` → `Deseu`, `Vols` → `Voleu`, `els teus` → `els vostres`). Distinguishes descriptions (3rd person, untouched) from commands (imperatives, converted).
- **Add 142 missing translation keys**:
  - All notifications (`quota_warning_notification_*`, `session_depleted_notification_*`, `session_restored_notification_*`, `login_success_notification_*`)
  - All `Manual cleanup: *` headers and `Clearing removes *` bodies
  - All MiniMax services (`minimax_service_*`, `minimax_usage_amount_format`, `minimax_used_percent_format`)
  - Pace / run-out states (`On pace`, `Runs out in %@`, `Projected empty`, `Pace: %@`, `%d%% in deficit/reserve`)
  - Basic UI (`About CodexBar`, `Add Account...`, `Settings...`, `Quit`, `Switch Account...`, `Status Page`, `Usage Dashboard`, `Update ready, restart now?`)
  - Time formats (`Updated just now`, `today`, `just now`, `%dd`, `Last %d day`)
  - Provider errors (`Missing DeepSeek API key`, `No available fetch strategy for %@`, `OpenRouter API token not configured`, `z.ai API token not found`)
- **Fix typos and inconsistencies**:
  - `Clauers` → `Clauer` (16 occurrences, wrong plural)
  - `0 %` → `0%` (Catalan percent symbol has no space — current style was French)
  - `10 m` → `10 min` (`m` means metres)
  - `magatzem` → `emmagatzematge` (terminology unification)
  - `OK` → `D'acord` in dialogs
  - `Ús utilitzat` → `Ús consumit` (redundancy)
  - `Copia conserva` → `l'opció Copia conserva` (grammar)
  - Token count mismatch in `minimax_usage_amount_format` (1 → 2 tokens)

### Website strings (`docs/site-locales.mjs`)

- **Switch 'tu' → 'vós'** (e.g. `combina-los` → `combineu-los`)
- **Fix grammar/typos**:
  - `no s'emmagatzemen cap contrasenya` → `no s'emmagatzema cap contrasenya` (singular agreement)
  - `restableixen els comptes enrere` → `comptes enrere de reinici` (was nonsensical)
  - `Universal el GitHub Releases` → `Universal a GitHub Releases`
  - `es basen en cada versió` → `es publiquen a cada versió`
- **Align terminology with the app**: `Descarregar` → `Baixa` (castilianism), `Keychain` → `Clauer`, `Cookies` → `Galetes`, `Token` → `Testimoni`, `API key` → `Clau d'API`, `Legacy API` → `API antiga`, `enquesta d'estat` → `sondeig d'estat` (`enquesta` means questionnaire), `widgets` → `ginys`, `Construït per` → `Creat per`, `Guia d'autor` → `Guia de creació`.
- Use `programació amb IA` instead of `codificació d'IA` (more natural).

## Verification

- Current-main rewrite leaves the localization payload unchanged.
- `node Scripts/check-site-locales.mjs`: green.
- `node Scripts/check-app-locales.mjs`: green.
- `plutil` catalog validation: green.
- `make check`: green.
- `make test`: all 41 shards green; one transient shard 18 retry passed.
- Final current-main autoreview: clean (0.86).

## Notes

- The `ca` catalog now has **1051 keys matching the English source exactly** (0 missing, 0 extra).
- The address form was already mixed before this PR (mostly 'tu'); this PR makes it consistently 'vós' as recommended by Softcatalà. Post-review follow-up commits also fixed LanguageTool grammar issues (double prepositions, anglicisms, gerund periphrasis) and completed the vós conversion for sentence-level instructions. Button and menu labels (`Instal·la`, `Actualitza`, `Cerca`, `Afegeix`) are intentionally kept in singular imperative per Softcatalà UI guidelines.
- A few strings remain hardcoded in English in `CodexBarCore` (`"Daily Routines"`, `"Estimated from local Claude logs at API rates…"`) because that module has no access to the localization function `L()` defined in `CodexBar`. This is a known architectural limitation tracked separately and out of scope for this PR.

## Files changed

- `Sources/CodexBar/Resources/ca.lproj/Localizable.strings` (+395 −251)
- `docs/site-locales.mjs` (+35 −35)

target.title: fix(web-ui): skip hidden subagent picker pages
target.body:
Fixes #89249.

Summary:
- Advance the chat session picker through raw `sessions.list` pages that add no visible rows after hidden spawn-child subagent filtering.
- Stop showing raw total counts when the picker is filtering out loaded rows, avoiding misleading states like `1 / 177`.
- Add browser E2E regression coverage for a hidden-only page followed by a visible session page.

Surface area:
- No public config schema/options changes.
- No plugin API/contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surface.

Validation:
- `OPENCLAW_CAPTURE_UI_PROOF=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 passed; screenshot proof captured at `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` (1280x900 PNG).
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts -t "chat session picker" --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 6 passed, 89 skipped.
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> passed.
- `node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> 0 warnings/errors.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` -> passed.

Real behavior proof:
- Added a Playwright/Chromium Control UI E2E test that drives the actual chat picker in a browser against the mocked Gateway Control UI server.
- The test creates a visible first page plus a hidden subagent row, then clicks Load More. The mocked backend returns a hidden-only raw page at offset `2`; the picker automatically requests offset `4` and renders the next visible top-level chat.
- Assertions cover the requested offsets (`2`, then `4`), the visible picker count (`2`), absence of hidden subagent options, and removal of the Load More button after all visible rows are loaded.
- Proof environment: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Playwright Chromium, local `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` screenshot from the passing browser run.

Signed head:
- `c6b0342f48c4a67d475d40e35edd40110a95cbcc` (`gpgsig` present)

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.

target.title: docs(memory): align descriptors and docs with recursive memory/**/*.md
target.body:
## Summary
- The runtime already walks `memory/` recursively via `collectMemoryFilesFromDir` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`), so nested files like `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md` are indexed and searched. But several agent-facing descriptors and public docs still said `memory/*.md`, which can mislead agents into thinking the index is flat.
- This PR updates the remaining stale references to `memory/**/*.md`. No runtime behavior change.

## Files touched
- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` — lazy `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptors
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` — concrete `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptions
- `extensions/memory-core/src/prompt-section.ts` — system-prompt recall guidance
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — `agents.defaults.memorySearch` help text
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`, `memory.md`, `system-prompt.md`
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen`

## Note
This supersedes #72748. That PR also carried QMD watcher changes that were independently resolved upstream by #81802 (`watch-settle.ts`), so the surviving value was just this wording alignment. Splitting it out as a small, behavior-neutral PR for easier review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent-facing descriptors and public docs said `memory/*.md` even though the runtime already walks `memory/` recursively and indexes nested files such as `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows (a) the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, and (b) the after-patch descriptor and prompt strings on this branch actually carry the new `memory/**/*.md` wording when imported at runtime.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`. After-patch text proof imports the modified TypeScript modules directly from this branch via `node --import tsx` against working tree at HEAD `30f15f4e22`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

Step 4 — on the PR branch checkout (`30f15f4e22`), import the modified descriptor/help modules at runtime via `tsx` and print the after-patch strings to prove the new `memory/**/*.md` wording is observable from a real runtime call site (not just the diff):

```
node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs   # imports FIELD_HELP and buildPromptSection from this branch
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace, plus runtime-imported after-patch strings from this branch (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
0.812 memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md:1-9
    Recursive memory search proof.
    Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

After-patch text observed from this branch (HEAD `30f15f4e22`) by importing the modified modules at runtime:

```
$ node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs
=== schema.help.ts: FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch'] ===
Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search + memory_get }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts; then use memory_get to pull only the needed lines. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search only }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts and answer from the matching results. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== extensions/memory-core/index.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 127      description:
 128        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 139      description:
 140        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",

=== extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 240      description:
 241        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 410      description:
 411        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",
```

**Observed result after fix**: (a) The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. (b) Importing the modified `FIELD_HELP` map and `buildPromptSection` from this branch at runtime shows the after-patch strings carry `memory/**/*.md` for both the config help text and the system-prompt memory recall guidance, matching the diff exactly. (c) The two `memory_search` / `memory_get` description strings in `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` are inline string literals; the after-patch lines printed from this branch show the new wording in place. No runtime code paths are modified, only the descriptor and doc strings plus the regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:gen` regenerates baseline cleanly (no other drift)
- [x] Nested memory smoke search on installed `2026.5.20` returns the new nested file at score `0.812`
- [x] On-branch runtime import shows `FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch']`, `buildPromptSection(...)` output, and `memory_search` / `memory_get` description literals all carry `memory/**/*.md`
- [ ] CI: lint / type / config-baseline check

target.title: Clean up isolated cron sessions after runs
target.body:
Fixes #84707.

## Summary

- move isolated cron delete-after-run cleanup into a shared helper
- call delete-after-run cleanup from the isolated runner `finally` block, so delivery mode `none`, runner errors, and other terminal paths also delete run sessions
- keep the existing delivery-dispatch cleanup path using the shared helper

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated cron jobs with `deleteAfterRun: true` and `delivery.mode: "none"` must clean up their run session even though no delivery dispatch is requested.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw Gateway on Andy's Mac mini via `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, PR head `b2214e8b44e8cc7df9ffba4ac3a226d84d6c35a6`, worktree `/tmp/openclaw-proof-84794`; gateway token redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created and force-ran a real isolated cron job named `proof-84794-delete-after-run-delivery-none` with `deleteAfterRun: true`, `delivery.mode: "none"`, and an `agentTurn` payload. The finished run produced session id `29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d`, and `sessions.list` showed the retained cron session before applying this PR head's cleanup helper. Then ran:

```sh
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:18789 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<redacted> \
  node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs \
  proof-84794-cleanup-live.ts \
  agent:tech:cron:85d5ccb8-1526-4aaf-9623-05835871e5c6 \
  29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the command above:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "behavior": "cleanupCronRunSessionAfterRun deletes a delivery-none isolated cron session",
  "environment": {
    "branch": "proof-84794",
    "gateway": "local OpenClaw Gateway via ws://127.0.0.1:18789"
  },
  "before": [
    {
      "key": "agent:tech:cron:85d5ccb8-1526-4aaf-9623-05835871e5c6",
      "sessionId": "29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d"
    }
  ],
  "cleanupResult": true,
  "after": []
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `cleanupCronRunSessionAfterRun` returned `true`; the real Gateway `sessions.list` result contained the cron session before cleanup and returned an empty result afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Full Docker packaging was not rerun in this proof pass; the validation section below covers the focused source lane and format/type checks.

## Validation

- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fast-mode.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/cron/service.delivery-plan.test.ts src/cron/service.session-reaper-in-finally.test.ts`
- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node /Users/andy/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node /Users/andy/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...changed files...`

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`

commands.command: thank you for the thorough review and the detailed findings. let me address each p1 point: 1. **implicit `summarymodel` trust grant**: you're right. the normalization path that derives `llm` model-override policy from plugin-owned `summarymodel` config creates an unintended trust boundary expansion. i'll remove this implicit authorization path and keep model override trust under explicit `llm` policy only. 2. **`llm.allowmodeloverride: false` overridden**: good catch. the early-return check for explicit `true` misses the case where `allowmodeloverride` is explicitly set to `false` but gets overridden by the `summarymodel`-derived normalization. i'll fix the guard to respect explicit `false` and only grant trust when the operator has explicitly opted in. 3. **canonical fix selection**: acknowledged. i'll coordinate with the other candidate fixes (ref: #94067 canonical path context) and align with maintainers on the preferred allowlist-preserving lifecycle fix before pushing updates. note: the re-review has failed three times (runs [27739917104](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27739917104), [27740621781](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27740621781), [27751672187](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27751672187)). i'll push the revised implementation once the repair is complete and request re-review then. thanks again for the thorough analysis!
commands.freeform_prompt: Thank you for the thorough review and the detailed findings. Let me address each P1 point: 1. **Implicit `summaryModel` trust grant**: You're right. The normalization path that derives `llm` model-override policy from plugin-owned `summaryModel` config creates an unintended trust boundary expansion. I'll remove this implicit authorization path and keep model override trust under explicit `llm` policy only. 2. **`llm.allowModelOverride: false` overridden**: Good catch. The early-return check for explicit `true` misses the case where `allowModelOverride` is explicitly set to `false` but gets overridden by the `summaryModel`-derived normalization. I'll fix the guard to respect explicit `false` and only grant trust when the operator has explicitly opted in. 3. **Canonical fix selection**: Acknowledged. Note: The re-review has failed three times (runs [27739917104](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27739917104), [27740621781](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27740621781), [27751672187](https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27751672187)). I'll push the revised implementation once the repair is complete and request re-review then. Thanks again for the thorough analysis!

target.title: fix: auto-grant lossless-claw model override from summaryModel config
target.body:
## Summary

Move the lossless-claw `llm` policy auto-population from the runtime LLM resolution path into the plugin config normalization layer (`normalizePluginsConfig`). This ensures `summaryModel`-derived override trust is populated at config load time — matching `ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` in `codex-route-warnings.ts` but applying to every normal config load, not just legacy migration.

## Problem

`ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` only runs during legacy config migration (`doctor --fix`). For users with a modern plugin-entry config that has `summaryModel` but no `llm` block, the override trust is never granted at startup. LCM compaction silently falls back to deterministic truncation instead of using the configured LLM summarizer.

The original v1 fallback in `resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy` bypassed the documented `plugins.entries.<id>.llm` trust gate and was flagged by ClawSweeper.

## Fix

- **`src/plugins/config-state.ts`** (+56): Added `normalizeLosslessLlmPolicy`, called from `normalizePluginsConfig`. Auto-populates `llm: { allowModelOverride: true, allowedModels: [summaryModel] }` when `config.summaryModel` is set but no explicit `llm` block exists. Identical logic to `ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` in `codex-route-warnings.ts`.

- **`src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.ts`** (-40 +2): Removed runtime `summaryModel` fallback. `resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy` now reads `normalizePluginsConfig(cfg.plugins).entries[pluginId]?.llm` — the normalized config carries the correct policy.

### Why this approach

1. Config normalization happens once at load time, not per-`complete()` call
2. `llm.allowModelOverride` remains the single trust gate — `summaryModel` populates it rather than bypassing it
3. Consistent with existing `doctor --fix` migration (`ensureLosslessLlmPolicy`)
4. Explicit `llm` block takes priority when configured

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Lossless-claw LCM compaction silently degrades to deterministic truncation fallback when `summaryModel` is configured without an explicit `llm` block. The `summaryModel → llm` auto-population only runs during `doctor --fix` migration, not at normal config load, so override trust is never granted at startup.

**Real environment tested**: 
- Real OpenClaw Gateway, Node.js v26.2.0, Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112)
- Build from `fix/issue-94289-lossless-llm-override` branch
- lossless-claw plugin v0.13.1, loaded from dist extensions directory
- Live model provider: `stepfun-plan` (`https://api.stepfun.com/step_plan/v1`), model `step-3.5-flash`, via `http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80`
- Test config: `/tmp/lcm-before-test/openclaw.json`, port 18793, token auth
- `slots.contextEngine: lossless-claw`, `contextThreshold: 0.05`, `proactiveThresholdCompactionMode: inline`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Build OpenClaw with the fix applied
2. Start gateway with config containing `summaryModel` but no explicit `llm` block:
   ```bash
   nohup https_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 http_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
     node /home/0668001050/workspace/pr-killer/openclaw/dist/index.js \
     run --state-dir /tmp/lcm-before-test --verbose > /tmp/lcm-after-gateway.log 2>&1 &
   ```
3. Connect webchat UI at `http://127.0.0.1:18793`
4. Send ~70K+ tokens of conversation to trigger LCM compaction (contextThreshold=0.05 ensures early trigger)
5. Monitor gateway logs for LCM compaction entries

**Evidence after fix**:
```
[lcm] incremental compaction decision: shouldCompact=true, reason=budget-trigger
[lcm] compactLeafAsync start: maxPasses=7, allowCondensedPasses=true
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 142810 → 128087
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 128087 → 108648
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 108648 → 106568
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 106568 → 86344
```

Config normalization import test (importing production `dist/` modules directly):
```
Config: lossless-claw entry, summaryModel="stepfun-plan/step-3.5-flash", no llm block

BEFORE (normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver, no fix):
  llm.allowModelOverride: undefined
  llm.allowedModels:     []
  → override denied

AFTER (normalizePluginsConfig, with fix):
  llm.allowModelOverride: true
  llm.hasAllowedModelsConfig: true
  llm.allowedModels:     ["stepfun-plan/step-3.5-flash"]
  → override granted
```

BEFORE evidence (fix disabled in dist, same config):
```
[lcm] incremental compaction decision: rawTokensOutsideTail=0, shouldCompact=false
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (fallback): 67847 → 67639
```
LLM summarizer never called. LCM degraded to deterministic truncation with near-zero compression (200 tokens, 0.3%).

**Observed result after fix**: All 4 leaf passes used LLM summarizer at `normal` level. Total compression: 142,810 → 86,344 tokens (40% reduction). Zero "model override denied" errors. The `summaryModel → llm` auto-population at config load time correctly grants model override trust, and the LCM plugin successfully invokes the configured LLM summarizer.

| Metric | BEFORE (no fix) | AFTER (with fix) |
|--------|----------------|-------------------|
| Compaction level | `fallback` (deterministic) | `normal` (LLM) |
| Token reduction | 200 (0.3%) | 56,466 (40%) |
| Summaries created | 1 | 4 |
| Model override denied | Silent fallback | 0 |

**What was not tested**: Other context engine plugins (only lossless-claw tested), other compaction modes beyond `inline`, other model providers beyond stepfun-plan.

### Full behavior chain

```
normalizePluginsConfig()
  ├─ BEFORE: normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver() only
  │   └─ entries["lossless-claw"].llm → undefined
  │       └─ resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy() → undefined
  │           └─ assertAllowedModelOverride() throws
  │               └─ LCM catches → deterministic fallback → near-zero compression
  │
  └─ AFTER: normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver() + normalizeLosslessLlmPolicy()
      └─ entries["lossless-claw"].llm → {allowModelOverride: true, allowedModels: [summaryModel]}
          └─ resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy() → valid policy
              └─ assertAllowedModelOverride() passes
                  └─ LCM calls LLM summarizer → 40% token reduction
```

## Unit tests (supplemental)

```
pnpm test src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.test.ts  → 21 passed
pnpm test src/plugins/config-state.test.ts                  → 54 passed (50 original + 4 regression)
pnpm test src/context-engine/                               → 18 passed
```

Fixes: #94289

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: fix(qmd): preserve Windows absolute paths in command resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #92302 — `memory.qmd.command` path mangled on Windows, making QMD memory backend unusable on Windows despite working CLI.

`splitShellArgs` treats `\` as a POSIX shell escape character. When the configured QMD command is a Windows absolute path (e.g., `C:\Users\...\qmd.js`), every backslash is consumed as an escape, stripping path separators and producing an unspawnable command like `C:UsersuserAppData...qmd.js`.

This fix adds `isWindowsAbsoluteCommandPath()` to detect drive-letter (`C:\...`) and UNC (`\\server\...`) paths, and `extractQmdCommandToken()` to preserve the entire executable path intact for Windows absolute paths and bypass `splitShellArgs`. Since `memory.qmd.command` is documented as an executable path (not a shell command with args), the entire raw command string is returned as-is for Windows paths — including paths with spaces like `C:\Program Files\qmd\bin\qmd.js`.

## Changes

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts` — add `isWindowsAbsoluteCommandPath()` and `extractQmdCommandToken()` helpers; Windows absolute paths are preserved intact (spaces included), POSIX paths continue through `splitShellArgs` as before
- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts` — add regression tests for Windows drive-letter paths, UNC paths, and Windows paths with spaces

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `splitShellArgs` consumes every `\` as a POSIX shell escape outside quotes, mangling Windows absolute paths into unspawnable strings. Windows paths with spaces were also truncated at the first space before this fix.

**Real environment tested:** Node.js 22.22.0, Linux x64. The fix is pure string-tokenization logic — it only changes how the config parser extracts the executable path from `memory.qmd.command`. The downstream Windows spawn path through `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram` / `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram` is unchanged.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Construct Windows absolute paths as configured in `openclaw.json` on a Windows machine
2. Pass through `splitShellArgs` directly (before fix) — backslashes stripped
3. Pass through `extractQmdCommandToken` (after fix) — path preserved
4. Verify POSIX quoted path fallback still works
5. Verify Windows path with spaces is preserved (not truncated at first space)

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== BEFORE fix (splitShellArgs directly) ===
Input:  C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js
Output: C:UsersuserAppDataRoamingnpmnode_modules@tobiluqmddistcliqmd.js
→ PATH MANGLED, unspawnable

=== AFTER fix (extractQmdCommandToken) ===
Input:  C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js
Output: C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js
→ PATH PRESERVED

=== Windows path with spaces (after fix) ===
Input:  C:\Program Files\qmd\bin\qmd.js
Output: C:\Program Files\qmd\bin\qmd.js
→ FULL PATH PRESERVED (no whitespace truncation)

=== UNC path (after fix) ===
Input:  \\server\share\qmd\qmd.js
Output: \\server\share\qmd\qmd.js
→ UNC PATH PRESERVED

=== POSIX quoted path still works ===
Input:  "/Applications/QMD Tools/qmd" --flag
Output: /Applications/QMD Tools/qmd
→ UNAFFECTED
```

**Observed result after fix:** Windows absolute paths (drive-letter, UNC, with/without spaces) are preserved intact. POSIX paths continue to work through `splitShellArgs` as before. No behavioral change for non-Windows configurations.

**What was not tested:** Real Windows 11 spawn of `qmd.js` with this config end-to-end — requires a Windows machine with `@tobilu/qmd` installed. The original reporter's environment in #92302 provides real-world Windows confirmation. The fix changes only config-time string tokenization; the downstream Windows spawn path is unchanged.
